domain.name: Evolutionary Computation
domain.thesis: This first live slice tracks researcher-centered EC signals around open-source algorithm stacks, multiobjective optimization, and GPU-native evolutionary systems. The goal is to follow people who are not only publishing, but also shaping reusable infrastructure and realistic next-step directions.
questions: Which EC researchers are still pairing strong methods with durable code and infrastructure?
Where is the frontier moving from benchmark wins toward scalable, reusable systems?
Which recent papers look like genuine starting points for new contributions rather than isolated demonstrations?
watchSignals: recent TEVC, GECCO, and Swarm and Evolutionary Computation papers from tracked people
public repositories that still receive meaningful updates after publication
open-access PDFs that can be pulled automatically into the local archive

researchers.name: Ran Cheng
researchers.focus: GPU-native evolutionary systems, multiobjective optimization, and open-source EC infrastructure

researchers.name: Julian Blank
researchers.focus: parallel multiobjective optimization and practical optimization tooling

researchers.name: Kalyanmoy Deb
researchers.focus: multiobjective optimization, EC problem structure, and durable benchmark-setting ideas
